AnalysisBlock.description: ADR-002 §15–§19 — the shared analysis block used by every specialist.
AnalysisBlock.title: AnalysisBlock

Evidence.description:
A single evidence record (ADR-002 §14).

``type`` determines which fields are required; those requirements are enforced
by the modular rule registry defined below, not by inline conditionals.
Evidence.title: Evidence

EvidenceType.description: The four legal origins of a claim (ADR-002 §14).
EvidenceType.title: EvidenceType

Finding.description: A single specialist finding within an analysis block.
Finding.title: Finding

FrameworkSelection.description: ADR-002 §11 — a selected and adapted framework.
FrameworkSelection.title: FrameworkSelection

IssueNode.description: ADR-002 §12 — a node in the (flat, parent-referenced) issue tree.
IssueNode.title: IssueNode

LifecycleStatus.description: The engagement state-machine positions defined in ADR-002 §2.
LifecycleStatus.title: LifecycleStatus

PendingRequirement.description:
An outstanding requirement: an execution blocker or missing information.

Broadens the ADR-002 §2 ``blocked_on`` marker to represent both categories.
PendingRequirement.title: PendingRequirement

PhaseRecord.description: A phase entered (and optionally exited) during the engagement (ADR-002 §2).
PhaseRecord.title: PhaseRecord

PlanStep.description: A single step in the engagement plan (ADR-002 §10).
PlanStep.title: PlanStep

SensitivityCase.description: A single sensitivity case (a driver stressed to test the answer).
SensitivityCase.title: SensitivityCase

description:
Root Engagement State — the single source of truth (ADR-002).

Valid with only ``metadata``; all other sections default to empty/None and are
filled over the lifecycle.
title: EngagementState
